Guideline Action: Perform multiparametric MRI-targeted biopsy together with transrectal ultrasound systematic biopsy
Source Statement: If the MPMRI is positive, MPMRI-TB and TRUS-SB should be performed together to maximize detection of CSPCa.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: RECURRENCE_OR_RESTAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient had a prior negative transrectal ultrasound systematic biopsy and is at high risk of clinically significant prostate cancer with curative management being considered
Guideline Action: Perform multiparametric MRI
Source Statement: In patients who had a prior negative TRUS-SB and demonstrate a high risk of having CSPCa in whom curative management is being considered: MPMRI should be performed,

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: RECURRENCE_OR_RESTAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient had a prior negative transrectal ultrasound systematic biopsy and multiparametric MRI is positive
Guideline Action: Perform targeted biopsy
Source Statement: If the MPMRI is positive, targeted biopsy should be performed.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: Biopsy-naive patient has a negative multiparametric MRI
Guideline Action: Consider forgoing biopsy after discussing risks and benefits with the patient and arranging ongoing follow-up
Source Statement: If the MPMRI is negative, consider forgoing any biopsy after discussion of the risks and benefits with the patient as part of shared decision making and ongoing followup.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: Biopsy-naive patient has a negative multiparametric MRI
Guideline Action: Inform the patient that a negative multiparametric MRI may miss clinically significant prostate cancer
Source Statement: Between 8% and 24% of patients with CSPCa may be missed by a negative MPMRI.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: Considering multiparametric MRI for a biopsy-naive patient
Guideline Action: Use multiparametric MRI only if high-quality interpretation is available and operators have experience performing targeted biopsies
Source Statement: MPMRI should only be performed if there is availability of high-quality MPMRI interpretation and operators with experience performing targeted biopsies (see Recommendation 3).

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: Considering multiparametric MRI for a biopsy-naive patient
Guideline Action: Use multiparametric MRI only when there is intent for curative management if biopsy is positive
Source Statement: Due to the limited availability, MPMRI is recommended only for patients where there is intent of curative management should the biopsy be positive for CSPCa.
proposed_actions_text: - Perform multiparametric MRI prior to biopsy.

patient_vignette: A 42-year-old woman is 74 days after matched unrelated donor allogeneic stem cell transplant for acute myeloid leukemia. She developed diffuse erythematous rash, watery diarrhea, and mild transaminitis consistent with acute GVHD. Despite 10 days of high-dose prednisone, her diarrhea improved only slightly and the rash persists, so the transplant team considers her acute GVHD steroid-refractory. She remains hemodynamically stable and has no active infection. Because the GVHD is not responding adequately to steroids, the team is discussing next-line therapy.
proposed_actions: Consider extracorporeal photopheresis (ECP) as therapy for steroid-dependent or steroid-refractory acute GVHD.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Adult or paediatric patient after allogeneic transplant with steroid-dependent or steroid-refractory acute GVHD
Guideline Action: Consider extracorporeal photopheresis (ECP) as therapy
Source Statement: ECP is an acceptable therapy for the treatment of steroid-dependent / refractory acute GVHD in adult and paediatric patients

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Adult or paediatric patient after allogeneic transplant with steroid-dependent or steroid-refractory chronic GVHD
Guideline Action: Use extracorporeal photopheresis (ECP) as therapy
Source Statement: ECP is an effective therapy for the treatment of steroid-dependent / refractory chronic GVHD in adult and paediatric patients

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: Patient has steroid-refractory acute GVHD with primarily refractory skin involvement
Guideline Action: Consider extracorporeal photopheresis (ECP) treatment
Source Statement: patients with primarily refractory skin GVHD should be considered for ECP treatment

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 50
Recommendation Class: NO_RECOMMENDATION
Activation Condition: Patient has steroid-refractory GVHD and meets Ontario eligibility criteria
Guideline Action: Note that ECP is a covered therapy in Ontario
Source Statement: ECP is currently a covered therapy in Ontario for patients with steroid refractory GVHD who meet certain eligibility criteria
proposed_actions_text: - Consider extracorporeal photopheresis (ECP) as therapy for steroid-dependent or steroid-refractory acute GVHD.

patient_vignette: A 58-year-old woman underwent surgery for a 4.2 cm epithelial ovarian tumor found incidentally during evaluation for bloating. The operative report notes hysterectomy and bilateral salpingo-oophorectomy, but no pelvic or para-aortic lymph node sampling was performed, so the staging was suboptimal. Final pathology suggests stage I disease, but the report also states that the tumor morphology is somewhat unusual and the pathologist is not fully confident about the exact histologic subtype. She is otherwise recovering well and asks whether she needs more treatment now or whether additional surgery would help clarify the stage.
proposed_actions: Have the pathology reviewed by an expert.
Offer re-operation to optimally define the tumour stage and then give adjuvant therapy based on findings.
Offer platinum-based chemotherapy to decrease recurrence risk and improve survival.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: tumour pathology creates doubt
Guideline Action: Have the pathology reviewed by an expert
Source Statement: A tumour pathology causing doubt should be reviewed by an expert.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: RECURRENCE_OR_RESTAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: restaging of a sub-optimally staged patient reveals more advanced disease
Guideline Action: Use chemotherapy as the preferred treatment option
Source Statement: If the restaging of a sub-optimally staged patient reveals a more advanced disease, chemotherapy is the preferred treatment option.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: lymph node sampling was not conducted as part of staging surgery
Guideline Action: Favor adjuvant chemotherapy
Source Statement: A subgroup analysis of one randomized controlled trial demonstrated that if lymph node sampling is not conducted as part of the staging surgery then adjuvant chemotherapy is favoured in terms of overall survival.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: patient has undergone optimal surgical staging including pelvic and para-aortic lymph node sampling and has stage I disease
Guideline Action: Consider adjuvant platinum-based chemotherapy
Source Statement: Women who have undergone optimal surgical staging, including pelvic and para-aortic lymph node sampling, and have stage I disease may or may not benefit from adjuvant platinum-based chemotherapy.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: patient has not undergone optimal surgical staging
Guideline Action: Offer re-operation to optimally define tumour stage and then give adjuvant therapy based on findings
Source Statement: Women who have not undergone optimal surgical staging can be offered two options. The first option is that they undergo re-operation to optimally define the tumour stage and then be offered adjuvant therapy based on the findings.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: patient has not undergone optimal surgical staging
Guideline Action: Offer platinum-based chemotherapy to decrease recurrence risk and improve survival
Source Statement: Women who have not undergone optimal surgical staging can be offered two options. The other option is that they be offered platinum-based chemotherapy to decrease the risk of recurrence and improve survival.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: reoperation confirms stage I disease after suboptimal staging
Guideline Action: Base the adjuvant chemotherapy decision on discussion with the patient about potential benefits and risks
Source Statement: The treatment decision must be based on a discussion with the patient about potential benefits and risks.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 50
Recommendation Class: NO_RECOMMENDATION
Activation Condition: considering adjuvant pelvic radiation
Guideline Action: Do not make a recommendation because evidence is insufficient
Source Statement: There is insufficient evidence to make a recommendation on the role of adjuvant pelvic radiation, whole abdominal-pelvic radiotherapy, or intraperitoneal radioactive chromic phosphate.
proposed_actions_text:
- Have the pathology reviewed by an expert.
- Offer re-operation to optimally define the tumour stage and then give adjuvant therapy based on findings.
- Offer platinum-based chemotherapy to decrease recurrence risk and improve survival.

patient_vignette: A 29-year-old woman presents to a community health clinic for an overdue cervical cancer screening. She recently moved to the area, does not have an established primary care physician, and says the clinic’s schedule is full for the next two months. She is sexually active, has no prior abnormal Pap results, and is due for routine screening. She asks whether the visit can be completed today because taking time off work is difficult and she is concerned about delaying care. The clinic has an experienced nurse practitioner available in an alternate provider role and routinely serves patients with limited access to screening services.
proposed_actions: Use NPs in alternate provider roles to improve access to breast and cervical cancer screening.
Allow NPs to perform Pap smears as alternate providers to physicians.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: primary care or community setting and need to improve breast or cervical cancer screening access
Guideline Action: Use NPs in alternate provider roles to improve access to breast and cervical cancer screening.
Source Statement: In primary care and community-based settings, NPs working in alternate provider roles can be utilized to improve access to breast and cervical cancer screening.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: need for Pap smear screening
Guideline Action: Allow NPs to perform Pap smears as alternate providers to physicians.
Source Statement: Two studies demonstrated that the quality of Papanicolaou (Pap) smears conducted by NPs was not detectably different and, in some cases, was superior to physicians (23,24).

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: need for esophagoscopy, flexible sigmoidoscopy, or colonoscopy for cancer screening
Guideline Action: Use NPs as alternate providers to physicians for esophagoscopy, flexible sigmoidoscopy, and colonoscopy.
Source Statement: As alternate providers to physicians, NPs can provide safe and effective care in performing esophagoscopy, flexible sigmoidoscopy, and colonoscopy for cancer screening.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: woman with cervical dysplasia needing colposcopy-guided biopsy to diagnose cervical cancer
Guideline Action: Use NPs as alternate providers to physicians for colposcopy-guided biopsies.
Source Statement: For women with cervical dysplasia, NPs are an appropriate alternate provider to physicians in performing colposcopy-guided biopsies to diagnose cervical cancer.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: newly diagnosed cancer patient receiving surgery or radiation therapy and CNS-led alternate model is being considered
Guideline Action: Use CNS-led care as an appropriate alternate model to physician care.
Source Statement: Clinical nurse specialist (CNS)-led care is an appropriate alternate model to care provided by physicians, particularly for newly diagnosed patients undergoing surgery or radiation therapy.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: new diagnosis of cancer and post cancer surgery or receiving chemotherapy or radiation treatment
Guideline Action: Consider adding complementary CNS care to usual care to improve psychological and mental well-being and possibly survival.
Source Statement: The addition of complementary CNS care to usual care may improve psychological and mental well-being and survival for patients with a new diagnosis of cancer who are post cancer surgery or receiving chemotherapy or radiation treatment.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: breast or colorectal cancer follow-up and telephone follow-up is being considered
Guideline Action: Consider CNS- or NP-delivered telephone follow-up as a safe and acceptable alternate model to outpatient physician follow-up.
Source Statement: For patients with breast and colorectal cancer, CNS-or NP-delivered telephone follow-up may provide a safe and acceptable alternate model to outpatient clinic follow-up care provided mostly by physicians.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: woman following breast cancer treatment with menopausal symptoms
Guideline Action: Consider a complementary NP-led comprehensive assessment and intervention program to reduce menopausal symptoms.
Source Statement: The addition of a complementary and comprehensive assessment and intervention program provided by a NP may be effective for reducing menopausal symptoms in women following treatment for breast cancer.
proposed_actions_text:
- Use NPs in alternate provider roles to improve access to breast and cervical cancer screening.
- Allow NPs to perform Pap smears as alternate providers to physicians.

patient_vignette: A 58-year-old woman is diagnosed with ductal carcinoma in situ (DCIS) after a screening mammogram showed clustered microcalcifications in the upper outer quadrant of the left breast. Core biopsy confirms intermediate-grade DCIS. Breast imaging shows a 2.1 cm localized area without multifocal disease, and the breast surgeon feels she is a good candidate for breast-conserving surgery. The patient is anxious about recurrence but strongly prefers to preserve her breast if oncologically appropriate. She asks about treatment options and whether radiation would still be needed after surgery.
proposed_actions: Offer her the choice of breast-conserving surgery or total mastectomy.
After breast-conserving surgery, offer adjuvant breast irradiation.
Consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it reduces invasive and non-invasive ipsilateral recurrence.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: woman has DCIS and is a candidate for breast-conserving surgery
Guideline Action: offer choice of breast-conserving surgery or total mastectomy
Source Statement: Women with DCIS of the breast who are candidates for breast-conserving surgery should be offered the choice of breast-conserving surgery or total mastectomy.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: breast-conserving surgery is performed for DCIS
Guideline Action: remove all mammographically suspicious calcifications and ensure microscopically clear margins of DCIS
Source Statement: When breast-conserving surgery is performed, all mammographically suspicious calcifications should be removed and margins should be microscopically clear of DCIS.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: DCIS is large enough that breast-conserving surgery would leave an unacceptable cosmetic result
Guideline Action: recommend mastectomy with option of reconstruction
Source Statement: Mastectomy, with the option of reconstruction, is recommended for those women who have an area of DCIS large enough that breast-conserving surgery would leave them with an unacceptable cosmetic result.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: DCIS treated with breast-conserving surgery and whole-breast irradiation
Guideline Action: aim for at least 2 mm margin to reduce local recurrence risk
Source Statement: A 2016 joint guideline from the Society of Surgical Oncology (SSO), American Society of Radiation Oncology (ASTRO), and American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) recommends margins of at least 2 mm to reduce the risk of local recurrence.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: DCIS treated with breast-conserving surgery and whole-breast irradiation and margin is positive (ink on DCIS)
Guideline Action: recognize positive margin as associated with significant risk of ipsilateral breast tumour recurrence
Source Statement: For patients with DCIS treated with breast-conserving surgery and whole-breast irradiation, a positive margin (ink on DCIS) is associated with a significant risk of ipsilateral breast tumour recurrence (IBTR).

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: woman has DCIS and has undergone breast-conserving surgery
Guideline Action: offer adjuvant breast irradiation
Source Statement: Women with DCIS who have undergone breast-conserving surgery should be offered adjuvant breast irradiation.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: DCIS treated with breast-conserving surgery
Guideline Action: consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it reduces invasive and non-invasive ipsilateral recurrence
Source Statement: In each, the risk of invasive and non-invasive ipsilateral recurrence was reduced with adjuvant radiotherapy.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: woman prefers to maximize local control
Guideline Action: consider mastectomy with option for reconstruction
Source Statement: Mastectomy with the option for reconstruction remains an acceptable choice for women preferring to maximize local control.
proposed_actions_text:
- Offer her the choice of breast-conserving surgery or total mastectomy.
- After breast-conserving surgery, offer adjuvant breast irradiation.
- Consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it reduces invasive and non-invasive ipsilateral recurrence.

patient_vignette: A 64-year-old man with newly diagnosed adenocarcinoma of the sigmoid colon undergoes colonoscopy after several months of intermittent hematochezia and iron-deficiency anemia. Biopsy confirms colorectal cancer, and contrast-enhanced CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis shows a localized cT3N1 lesion without definite distant metastases. His oncology team discusses staging before surgery, and the patient asks whether a PET scan would better define the extent of disease. He has no prior treatment and no symptoms suggesting recurrence or metastatic complications. The team plans standard surgical and oncologic management based on the available anatomic imaging and pathology.
proposed_actions: Do not use routine PET for diagnosis or staging of this clinical stage I-III colorectal cancer.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: clinical stage I-III colorectal cancer
Guideline Action: Do not use routine PET for diagnosis or staging.
Source Statement: The routine use of PET is not recommended for the diagnosis or staging of clinical Stage I-III colorectal cancers.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: TREATMENT_RESPONSE
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: locally advanced rectal cancer before and after preoperative chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Do not routinely use PET to measure treatment response.
Source Statement: The routine use of PET is not recommended for the measurement of treatment response in locally advanced rectal cancer before and after preoperative chemotherapy.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: TREATMENT_RESPONSE
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: short-term response assessment after chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Do not use PET as a substitute for CT.
Source Statement: The study suggested that PET should not be used as a substitute for CT for short-term response and should not be used as a surrogate for long-term clinical endpoints.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: TREATMENT_RESPONSE
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: long-term clinical endpoint assessment such as time to progression or overall survival
Guideline Action: Do not use PET as a surrogate for long-term clinical endpoints.
Source Statement: The study suggested that PET should not be used as a substitute for CT for short-term response and should not be used as a surrogate for long-term clinical endpoints.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: TREATMENT_RESPONSE
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: one-time PET after preoperative therapy to predict complete pathologic response
Guideline Action: Do not rely on a single post-therapy PET for predicting complete pathologic response.
Source Statement: However, one-time PET after preoperative therapy poorly predicted complete pathologic response after preoperative therapy in Capirci et al (13) and Kalff et al (14) and poorly predicted posttherapy staging in Capirci et al (12).

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: TREATMENT_RESPONSE
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: after chemotherapy when deciding on liver resection
Guideline Action: Do not use PET in decision making for liver resection.
Source Statement: The study showed that ultrasonography also guides surgical decision during intraoperative assessment and suggested PET after chemotherapy should not be used in decision making for liver resection.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: TREATMENT_RESPONSE
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: single PET/CT after preoperative therapy to predict complete pathologic response
Guideline Action: Do not rely on a single PET/CT after therapy for predicting complete pathologic response.
Source Statement: Kristiansen et al ( 17) suggested a single PET/CT after preoperative therapy poorly predicted complete pathologic response.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: RECURRENCE_OR_RESTAGING
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: routine surveillance after curative surgery in patients at high risk for recurrence
Guideline Action: Do not use PET routinely for surveillance.
Source Statement: PET is not recommended for routine surveillance in patients with colorectal cancer treated with curative surgery at high risk for recurrence.
proposed_actions_text: - Do not use routine PET for diagnosis or staging of this clinical stage I-III colorectal cancer.

patient_vignette: A 62-year-old woman presents with a 3.2 cm unifocal squamous cell carcinoma of the left vulva. On exam, the groin nodes are enlarged, firm, and mildly tender bilaterally, making them clinically suspicious for metastasis. She has no prior groin surgery and is otherwise fit for treatment. The gynecologic oncology team is discussing the next step in nodal staging, and the patient asks whether a sentinel lymph node biopsy could be done to avoid a larger dissection.
proposed_actions: Do not perform sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB) because clinically suspicious groin nodes are present.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: clinically suspicious groin nodes are present
Guideline Action: do not perform SLNB
Source Statement: SLNB is not recommended when there are clinically suspicious groin nodes.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: considering blue dye alone for SLNB mapping
Guideline Action: do not use blue dye alone
Source Statement: Blue dye alone should be discouraged because of its low detection rate.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: considering use of blue dye alone because of low detection rate
Guideline Action: avoid blue dye alone
Source Statement: Blue dye alone should be discouraged because of its low detection rate.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: woman with unifocal vulvar squamous cell tumour smaller than 4 cm and clinically nonsuspicious groin nodes
Guideline Action: perform sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB)
Source Statement: SLNB is recommended for women with unifocal tumours <4 cm in size and clinically nonsuspicious nodes in the groin.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: performing SLNB for early-stage vulvar cancer
Guideline Action: ensure appropriate surgical training with supervised SLNB experience, complete IFLD follow-up without false negatives, and ongoing annual experience
Source Statement: Appropriate surgical training (i.e., supervised experiences with SLNB procedures followed by complete IFLD without any false negatives and ongoing annual experience with cases to maintain competence) is recommended to optimize patient outcomes and safety.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: performing SLNB for early-stage vulvar cancer
Guideline Action: have the procedure performed by gynecologic oncologists in Gynecologic Oncology Centres
Source Statement: This procedure should be performed by gynecologic oncologists in Gynecologic Oncology Centres.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: centre performing SLNB for vulvar cancer
Guideline Action: ensure at least 10 successful SLNB followed by IFLD cases per centre
Source Statement: Although volume has not been explicitly studied, the Working Group agrees that successful experience with SLNB followed by IFLD in at least 10 patients per centre is recommended.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: performing SLNB mapping
Guideline Action: use radiocolloid tracers alone or with blue dye
Source Statement: Radiocolloid tracers should be used alone or with blue dye.
proposed_actions_text: - Do not perform sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB) because clinically suspicious groin nodes are present.

patient_vignette: A 59-year-old man with a recently diagnosed mid-rectal adenocarcinoma is being evaluated for possible neoadjuvant treatment. He has mild intermittent rectal bleeding but no bowel obstruction. On digital rectal exam, the tumor is palpable on the posterior rectal wall, about 7 cm from the anal verge, with reduced mobility. The oncology team is considering preoperative chemoradiation because the lesion may be locally advanced, but the staging workup is not yet complete. He has had no dedicated pelvic MRI, no endorectal ultrasound, and no CT of the abdomen or chest imaging. The patient asks whether the tumor is definitely stage III, and the team explains that preoperative staging can be imperfect and the final extent may differ from initial tests.
proposed_actions: Perform adequate preoperative staging investigations, including transrectal ultrasound and/or MRI for T category, MRI for N category, digital rectal exam, CT or MRI to assess the mesorectal margin, CT or MRI of the abdomen for metastatic or stage IV disease, and chest x-ray for pulmonary imaging.
Ensure preoperative therapy recommendations are applied only after adequate staging investigations have been completed.
Discuss the potential inaccuracies of preoperative testing on tumor staging with the patient to support informed decision-making.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: PREOP_STAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Before preoperative therapy is planned
Guideline Action: Perform adequate preoperative staging investigations including transrectal ultrasound and/or MRI for T category, MRI for N category, digital rectal exam, CT or MRI to assess mesorectal margin, CT or MRI of the abdomen for metastatic or stage IV disease, and chest x-ray for pulmonary imaging.
Source Statement: Recommendations for preoperative therapy presuppose adequate preoperative staging investigations, including transrectal ultrasound and/or magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) with surface or endorectal coil to assess the T category, MRI with surface or endorectal coil to assess the N category, a good digital rectal exam, computerized axial tomography (CAT) scan or MRI to assess the mesorectal margin, CAT scan or MRI of the abdomen to assess for potential metastatic or stage IV disease, and chest x-ray for pulmonary imaging.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: PREOP_STAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: When preoperative staging may be inaccurate
Guideline Action: Discuss potential inaccuracies of preoperative testing on tumor staging with the patient to support informed decision-making.
Source Statement: Potential inaccuracies of preoperative testing on tumour staging should be discussed with patients to allow them to make informed decisions (1).

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: PREOP_STAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: When planning rectal surgery after preoperative therapy
Guideline Action: Use total mesorectal excision (TME) principles for the eventual rectal surgery.
Source Statement: The eventual rectal surgery is expected to include total mesorectal excision (TME) principles.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: PREOP_STAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: When considering preoperative treatment benefits
Guideline Action: Recognize that the quality of surgery strongly influences the potential benefits of preoperative treatments.
Source Statement: The quality of surgery greatly influences the potential benefits of preoperative treatments.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: PREOP_STAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: When evaluating preoperative treatment evidence
Guideline Action: Account for the fact that many trials did not use currently recommended surgical standards, including TME.
Source Statement: A substantial number of trials included in the evidentiary base did not use currently recommended standards of surgery, including TME.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: PREOP_STAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: When preoperative therapy is being considered
Guideline Action: Ensure preoperative therapy recommendations are applied only after adequate staging investigations have been completed.
Source Statement: Recommendations for preoperative therapy presuppose adequate preoperative staging investigations...

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: PREOP_STAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: When discussing preoperative therapy with the patient
Guideline Action: Discuss the potential inaccuracies of staging tests so the patient can make an informed choice.
Source Statement: Potential inaccuracies of preoperative testing on tumour staging should be discussed with patients to allow them to make informed decisions (1).

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: PREOP_STAGING
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: When planning surgery after preoperative RT
Guideline Action: Plan surgery with TME principles in mind.
Source Statement: The eventual rectal surgery is expected to include total mesorectal excision (TME) principles.
proposed_actions_text:
- Perform adequate preoperative staging investigations, including transrectal ultrasound and/or MRI for T category, MRI for N category, digital rectal exam, CT or MRI to assess the mesorectal margin, CT or MRI of the abdomen for metastatic or stage IV disease, and chest x-ray for pulmonary imaging.
- Ensure preoperative therapy recommendations are applied only after adequate staging investigations have been completed.
- Discuss the potential inaccuracies of preoperative testing on tumor staging with the patient to support informed decision-making.

patient_vignette: A 52-year-old man in Ontario presents with a newly diagnosed, biopsy-proven undifferentiated nasopharyngeal carcinoma. Staging MRI and PET-CT show a stage IVB locally advanced primary tumor with bilateral cervical nodal involvement but no distant metastases. He has good performance status, normal renal and hepatic function, and is considered chemotherapy-eligible. The oncology team plans induction chemotherapy followed by definitive chemoradiotherapy. He understands the treatment will be coordinated closely because the timing of the transition to radiation is important after induction therapy.
proposed_actions: Use a chemoradiotherapy-based management approach for this newly diagnosed, chemotherapy-eligible stage IV locally advanced nasopharyngeal cancer.
If induction chemotherapy is given, administer it every 3 weeks for a total of 3 cycles; at minimum, 2 cycles are acceptable.
Start chemoradiotherapy within 21 to 28 days from the first day of the last induction chemotherapy cycle.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: newly diagnosed, chemotherapy-eligible, locally advanced squamous cell or undifferentiated nasopharyngeal cancer (stage III or IV)
Guideline Action: Use chemoradiotherapy-based management with consideration of adding neoadjuvant, concurrent, or adjuvant chemotherapy as addressed in the guideline
Source Statement: The objectives of the guideline are to make recommendations regarding the addition of neoadjuvant/concurrent/adjuvant chemotherapy to chemoradiotherapy in the management of locally advanced squamous cell or undifferentiated nasopharyngeal cancer (NPC).

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patients with NPC receiving induction chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Administer induction chemotherapy every 3 weeks for 3 cycles; at minimum give 2 cycles
Source Statement: For patients with NPC receiving induction chemotherapy, the regimens should be administered every three weeks for a total of three cycles, or at the minimum two cycles.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patients with NPC receiving induction chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Start chemoradiotherapy within 21 to 28 days from the first day of the last induction chemotherapy cycle
Source Statement: For patients with NPC receiving induction chemotherapy, chemoradiotherapy should be commenced within 21-28 days from the first day of the last cycle of induction chemotherapy.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: all patients with NPC receiving adjuvant chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Offer PF every 4 weeks for a total of 3 cycles
Source Statement: For all patients with NPC receiving adjuvant chemotherapy, PF ... administered every four weeks for a total of three cycles should be offered.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patients with locally advanced NPC treated in Ontario context
Guideline Action: Apply the endorsed CSCO/ASCO recommendations as modified by Ontario Health endorsement
Source Statement: The Chemotherapy and Radiotherapy for Nasopharyngeal Cancer Development Group (GDG) of Ontario Health (Cancer Care Ontario) endorses the Chinese Society of Clinical Oncology (CSCO)/American Society of Clinical Oncology (ASCO) recommendations ... modified by the endorsement process described in this document.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patients with NPC receiving induction chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Use a 3-week cycle interval for induction chemotherapy
Source Statement: For patients with NPC receiving induction chemotherapy, the regimens should be administered every three weeks for a total of three cycles, or at the minimum two cycles.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patients with NPC receiving adjuvant chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Use a 4-week cycle interval for PF adjuvant chemotherapy
Source Statement: For all patients with NPC receiving adjuvant chemotherapy, PF ... administered every four weeks for a total of three cycles should be offered.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patients with NPC receiving induction chemotherapy followed by chemoradiotherapy
Guideline Action: Do not delay chemoradiotherapy beyond 28 days after the first day of the last induction cycle
Source Statement: For patients with NPC receiving induction chemotherapy, chemoradiotherapy should be commenced within 21-28 days from the first day of the last cycle of induction chemotherapy.
proposed_actions_text:
- Use a chemoradiotherapy-based management approach for this newly diagnosed, chemotherapy-eligible stage IV locally advanced nasopharyngeal cancer.
- If induction chemotherapy is given, administer it every 3 weeks for a total of 3 cycles; at minimum, 2 cycles are acceptable.
- Start chemoradiotherapy within 21 to 28 days from the first day of the last induction chemotherapy cycle.

patient_vignette: A 58-year-old man is seen in clinic for a new guideline review after his primary care physician asked for oncology guidance on a recently diagnosed condition. The document available for reference contains only the front matter, section headings, and copyright/contact information, without any disease-specific management content or treatment recommendations. The patient’s history, exam, and test results are not being used to derive any intervention from this source because no clinical guidance is present in the document.
proposed_actions: No clinical action can be extracted from the provided text.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 50
Recommendation Class: NO_RECOMMENDATION
Activation Condition: When the document is used as a guideline reference
Guideline Action: No clinical action can be extracted from the provided text.
Source Statement: The provided text contains only document front matter, section headings, and copyright/contact information, with no clinical recommendations.
proposed_actions_text: - No clinical action can be extracted from the provided text.

patient_vignette: A 64-year-old woman with a 35-pack-year smoking history underwent right upper lobectomy for a 2.1 cm lung adenocarcinoma. Final pathology showed a completely resected stage IA (pT1bN0) non-small cell lung cancer with clear margins and no nodal involvement on systematic sampling. Postoperative recovery was uncomplicated, and she is now seen in oncology clinic 4 weeks later asking whether she should start chemotherapy to reduce recurrence risk. Her performance status is good, and she has no evidence of residual disease on imaging.
proposed_actions: Do not routinely give adjuvant chemotherapy.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has completely resected stage IA NSCLC
Guideline Action: do not routinely give adjuvant chemotherapy
Source Statement: The 2007 joint CCO/ASCO guideline recommended chemotherapy for stage II and IIIA disease but not stage IA.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has completely resected stage IB to IIIA NSCLC with sensitizing EGFR mutation
Guideline Action: do not use EGFR-TKI routinely as adjuvant therapy
Source Statement: These data were considered insufficient to justify the routine use of EGFR-TKIs in patients with tumors with sensitizing EGFR mutations.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has completely resected stage IB to IIIA NSCLC without EGFR mutation selection
Guideline Action: do not use erlotinib routinely as adjuvant therapy
Source Statement: No significant differences in DFS or OS were detected in the overall unselected study population.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has completely resected stage IB to IIIA NSCLC without EGFR mutation selection
Guideline Action: do not use gefitinib routinely as adjuvant therapy
Source Statement: There was no benefit for either of the subgroups with EGFR wild-type tumors or the 15 tumors (4% of the total sample) with EGFR sensitizing mutations.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has completely resected stage I to IIIA NSCLC
Guideline Action: do not use MAGE-A3 immunotherapy routinely as adjuvant treatment
Source Statement: MAGRIT... found no significant difference in the primary outcome DFS... Because there was no difference between groups, the study was unable to identify a biomarker that would enable selection of patients for this treatment.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has completely resected stage I to IIIA NSCLC
Guideline Action: do not use bevacizumab added to chemotherapy routinely as adjuvant therapy
Source Statement: a phase III RCT... found that the addition of angiogenesis inhibitor bevacizumab to chemotherapy failed to improve DFS or OS for individuals with surgically resected earlystage NSCLCs compared with chemotherapy alone.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has completely resected stage I or II NSCLC
Guideline Action: do not give postoperative radiotherapy (PORT) routinely
Source Statement: there has been no new evidence that would alter the recommendation against PORT in patients with stage I or II disease.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has completely resected stage II or IIIA NSCLC
Guideline Action: offer adjuvant cisplatin-based chemotherapy
Source Statement: Cisplatin-based adjuvant chemotherapy was recommended for routine use in patients with stage II or IIIA disease extent
proposed_actions_text: - Do not routinely give adjuvant chemotherapy.

patient_vignette: A 58-year-old man with a history of cutaneous melanoma on the left calf underwent wide local excision and sentinel lymph node biopsy. Pathology shows a single positive sentinel node with a small metastatic deposit, and staging scans show no distant disease. He is otherwise well and asks whether he should proceed with completion lymph node dissection (CLND) or be observed. The surgical team discusses that he is eligible for nodal observation with ultrasound rather than routine CLND, since the sentinel node metastasis is limited and ultrasound follow-up is feasible. He is comfortable with close surveillance and prefers to avoid the added morbidity of more extensive surgery.
proposed_actions: Do not pursue routine CLND when observation with ultrasonography is feasible, because CLND increases adverse events.
Consider nodal observation with ultrasonography rather than completion lymph node dissection for sentinel node metastasis.
Monitor the affected nodal basin with ultrasonography and clinical examination every 4 to 6 months for the first 2 years, then every 6 months from years 3 to 5.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: positive sentinel node and comparing CLND with observation
Guideline Action: Do not expect a survival benefit from CLND based on MSLT-II.
Source Statement: The non-significant difference in MSS and increase in adverse events of the CLND group indicates that CLND may not be optimal for patients and does not offer a survival benefit.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: positive sentinel node and comparing CLND with observation
Guideline Action: Do not expect benefit from CLND for any subgroup based on MSLT-II.
Source Statement: Although the majority of patients had low volume tumor metastases, sub set analysis did not demonstrate a benefit for any groups of patient receiving CLND.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: positive sentinel node and comparing CLND with observation
Guideline Action: Do not assume CLND improves distant metastasis-free survival, overall survival, or recurrence-free survival based on DeCOG-SLT.
Source Statement: The DeCOG-SLT trial found no difference in distant metastasis-free survival, overall survival, or recurrence-free survival when SLN positive patients who received CLND were compared to patients who were observed.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: positive sentinel node and considering CLND versus observation
Guideline Action: Avoid routine CLND when observation with ultrasonography is feasible because CLND increases adverse events.
Source Statement: Adverse events occurred with more frequency among the CLND patients than the observation group with lymphedema being the most common (24.1% of patients vs. 6.3% at last follow-up, p<0.001).

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient is managed with nodal observation after positive sentinel node
Guideline Action: Monitor the affected nodal basin with ultrasonography and clinical exam every 4 to 6 months for the first 2 years, then every 6 months from years 3 to 5.
Source Statement: Monitoring with ultrasonography of the affected nodal basin and clinical exam will be required, at minimum, every 4 to 6 months for the first 2 years and every 6 months from 3-5 years.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: ultrasound shows any two suspicious nodal features
Guideline Action: Confirm suspected nodal recurrence with biopsy of the basin.
Source Statement: Suspicions of nodal recurrence via ultrasound should be confirmed with a biopsy of the basin.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: patient has sentinel node metastasis
Guideline Action: Consider nodal observation with ultrasonography rather than completion lymph node dissection.
Source Statement: Patients with sentinel node metastasis should be considered for nodal observation with ultrasonography rather than CLND.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: patient has extensive sentinel node metastasis and CLND is the only option for local control
Guideline Action: Consider CLND as the better option than nodal observation with ultrasonography.
Source Statement: patients with extensive sentinel node metastasis in which CLND would be the only option for local control
proposed_actions_text:
- Do not pursue routine CLND when observation with ultrasonography is feasible, because CLND increases adverse events.
- Consider nodal observation with ultrasonography rather than completion lymph node dissection for sentinel node metastasis.
- Monitor the affected nodal basin with ultrasonography and clinical examination every 4 to 6 months for the first 2 years, then every 6 months from years 3 to 5.

patient_vignette: A 67-year-old man with dysphagia and a newly diagnosed localized mid-esophageal squamous cell carcinoma is evaluated by oncology. Staging shows T2N1 with a small-volume nodal burden and no distant metastases. He has an ECOG performance status of 1, maintains oral intake with soft foods, and has no major cardiac or renal comorbidities. After discussion of treatment options, he declines surgery and asks about curative non-surgical therapy. The team considers combined radiotherapy and chemotherapy, which he is felt likely to tolerate. Because he is otherwise fit, the oncologist discusses a concomitant approach rather than radiotherapy alone. The patient also asks about side effects and which chemotherapy backbone is usually used with radiation.
proposed_actions: Use concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy rather than radiotherapy alone.
Offer concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy as a curative-intent non-surgical approach.
Prefer a cisplatin-based chemotherapy regimen when concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy is used.
Inform the patient about increased acute toxicity associated with concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: sequential radiotherapy and chemotherapy is being considered
Guideline Action: Do not use sequential radiotherapy and chemotherapy as standard practice
Source Statement: Sequential radiotherapy and chemotherapy should not be recommended as standard practice.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: sequential radiotherapy and chemotherapy is being considered
Guideline Action: Avoid sequential radiotherapy and chemotherapy because no survival or local control benefit was shown and toxicity was increased
Source Statement: There was no evidence to suggest any survival or local control benefit using sequential RTCT compared with RT alone. Given these results and the increased adverse effects associated with sequential RTCT, this approach should not be recommended in standard practice.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with localized esophageal carcinoma, good performance status, considering non-surgical curative treatment, and combined radiotherapy and chemotherapy can be tolerated
Guideline Action: Use concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy rather than radiotherapy alone
Source Statement: Concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy is recommended over radiotherapy alone.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with localized esophageal carcinoma considering a non-surgical approach and combined radiotherapy and chemotherapy can be tolerated
Guideline Action: Consider concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy a reasonable option compared with radiotherapy alone
Source Statement: In patients with favourable performance status and who have a reasonable chance of completing concomitant RTCT, this approach is a reasonable option compared with radiotherapy alone.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient has favourable performance status and a reasonable chance of completing concomitant RTCT
Guideline Action: Offer concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy as a curative-intent non-surgical approach
Source Statement: The current review illustrates a benefit in local control and survival with the use of concomitant RTCT.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with localized (T1-3, small volume N1, M0) esophageal carcinoma and good performance status considering non-surgical treatment
Guideline Action: Apply the guideline recommendations to this patient group
Source Statement: These recommendations apply to adult patients with localized (T1-3, small volume N1, M0) carcinoma of the esophagus and good performance status who are considering a non-surgical approach and for whom combined radiotherapy and chemotherapy can be tolerated in the judgment of the treating oncologist.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: when concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy is being used
Guideline Action: Prefer a cisplatin-based chemotherapy regimen
Source Statement: a cisplatin-based chemotherapy regimen is a reasonable chemotherapy regimen to use when concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy is used.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: patient is being considered for concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Inform the patient about increased acute toxicity with this approach
Source Statement: Patients should be made aware of the increased acute toxicity associated with this approach.
proposed_actions_text:
- Use concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy rather than radiotherapy alone.
- Offer concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy as a curative-intent non-surgical approach.
- Prefer a cisplatin-based chemotherapy regimen when concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy is used.
- Inform the patient about increased acute toxicity associated with concomitant radiotherapy and chemotherapy.

patient_vignette: A 66-year-old man underwent radical prostatectomy for localized prostate adenocarcinoma. Final pathology shows pT3a disease with extracapsular extension and a focally positive posterior surgical margin. His postoperative PSA is currently undetectable, and he is recovering well with improving urinary continence. At follow-up, he asks whether he should wait for a PSA rise before doing anything further. The oncology team reviews the pathology and discusses postoperative management options, noting that his adverse pathologic features place him at higher risk of recurrence despite the undetectable PSA.
proposed_actions: Offer adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy.
Use adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy.
Consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it improves overall survival.
Consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it reduces distant metastases.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient has undergone radical prostatectomy and has pathologic T3 or margin-positive prostate cancer
Guideline Action: Offer adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy
Source Statement: In the only RCT for which long-term follow-up data are available, adjuvant RT following radical prostatectomy in patients with pathologic T3 or margin-positive prostate cancer has been shown to improve OS and reduce distant metastases compared to observation.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient has undergone radical prostatectomy and has positive surgical margins
Guideline Action: Offer adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy
Source Statement: The OS benefit extends individually to the subgroups with positive surgical margins, extracapsular extension, and seminal vesicle invasion.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient has undergone radical prostatectomy and has extracapsular extension
Guideline Action: Offer adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy
Source Statement: The OS benefit extends individually to the subgroups with positive surgical margins, extracapsular extension, and seminal vesicle invasion.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient has undergone radical prostatectomy and has seminal vesicle invasion
Guideline Action: Offer adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy
Source Statement: The OS benefit extends individually to the subgroups with positive surgical margins, extracapsular extension, and seminal vesicle invasion.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient has undergone radical prostatectomy and has any of positive surgical margins, extracapsular extension, or seminal vesicle invasion
Guideline Action: Use adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy
Source Statement: On the basis of the overall trial findings as well as these subgroup analyses, it can be concluded that men found at radical prostatectomy to have any of positive surgical margins, extracapsular extension, or seminal vesicle invasion benefit from adjuvant RT.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient is being considered for postoperative radiotherapy after prostatectomy and the comparison is observation with salvage therapy reserved
Guideline Action: Prefer adjuvant radiotherapy over observation with salvage therapy reserved
Source Statement: At a median follow-up of 12.6 years, adjuvant RT has now been shown to confer a significant benefit in terms of both OS and metastasis-free survival.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient is being considered for postoperative radiotherapy after prostatectomy
Guideline Action: Consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it improves overall survival
Source Statement: At a median follow-up of 12.6 years, adjuvant RT has now been shown to confer a significant benefit in terms of both OS and metastasis-free survival.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient is being considered for postoperative radiotherapy after prostatectomy
Guideline Action: Consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it reduces distant metastases
Source Statement: At a median follow-up of 12.6 years, adjuvant RT has now been shown to confer a significant benefit in terms of both OS and metastasis-free survival.
proposed_actions_text:
- Offer adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy.
- Use adjuvant radiotherapy after prostatectomy.
- Consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it improves overall survival.
- Consider adjuvant radiotherapy because it reduces distant metastases.

patient_vignette: A 62-year-old man with biopsy-proven localized prostate adenocarcinoma elected radical prostatectomy after counseling about management options and his preference for surgery. He has intermediate-risk disease, good baseline urinary control, and preserved erectile function. In the operating room, the surgeon is beginning the radical prostatectomy and plans to preserve continence and sexual function when oncologically appropriate. The team discusses the operative goals, including complete removal of the cancer-containing organ and achieving negative margins while balancing functional outcomes. Because the patient is not considered high-risk, nerve-sparing is being considered as part of the standard approach. The specimen is then handed off for routine pathologic processing.
proposed_actions: Aim to eradicate the cancer-containing organ with negative surgical margins.
Balance margin rates with functional outcomes.
Consider sparing the neurovascular bundles as the standard approach, since the patient is not high-risk.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: radical prostatectomy specimen is being handled in the operating room
Guideline Action: do not perform frozen section analysis for margin status
Source Statement: Frozen section analysis of the radical prostatectomy specimen (RPS) for margin status is not recommended.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: processing the apex of radical prostatectomy specimen
Guideline Action: do not use an en face shave technique at the apex
Source Statement: An en face (shave) technique is to be discouraged at the apex, as this approach can result in false-positive margin interpretation.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient is low-risk and surgery is the preferred option
Guideline Action: offer radical prostatectomy after full discussion and consideration of patient preferences
Source Statement: RP should be offered to low-risk and intermediate-risk patients for whom surgery is the preferred option after full discussion with patient and taking into account patient preferences.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient is intermediate-risk and surgery is the preferred option
Guideline Action: offer radical prostatectomy after full discussion and consideration of patient preferences
Source Statement: RP should be offered to low-risk and intermediate-risk patients for whom surgery is the preferred option after full discussion with patient and taking into account patient preferences.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: patient is high-risk
Guideline Action: offer referral for radiation consultation or review at a Multidisciplinary Cancer Conference (MCC)
Source Statement: High-risk patients should be offered a referral for radiation consultation or review at a Multidisciplinary Cancer Conference (MCC).

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: radical prostatectomy is being performed
Guideline Action: aim to eradicate the cancer-containing organ with negative surgical margins
Source Statement: The main goals of RP are (a) complete eradication of the cancer-containing organ with negative surgical margins, (b) preservation of urinary function, and (c) preservation of erectile function, where appropriate, but, in some cases, it is not possible to achieve all three.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: radical prostatectomy is being performed
Guideline Action: balance margin rates with functional outcomes
Source Statement: Radical Prostatectomy should aim at achieving a negative margin, while ensuring a balance between margin rates and functional outcomes

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: radical prostatectomy is being performed
Guideline Action: consider sparing the neurovascular bundles as the standard approach except in high-risk patients
Source Statement: Sparing of the neurovascular bundles should be considered the "standard approach" except for high-risk patients.
proposed_actions_text:
- Aim to eradicate the cancer-containing organ with negative surgical margins.
- Balance margin rates with functional outcomes.
- Consider sparing the neurovascular bundles as the standard approach, since the patient is not high-risk.

patient_vignette: A 72-year-old man with severe COPD and poor pulmonary reserve has a newly diagnosed 1.8 cm endobronchial squamous cell carcinoma in the right upper lobe bronchus. He is medically inoperable, and bronchoscopy shows the lesion is accessible endobronchially without evidence of invasion into a major vessel or a tracheoesophageal fistula. He has no history of porphyria or porphyrin allergy. Because the tumor is early stage and bronchoscopically accessible, photodynamic therapy is being considered. The team plans treatment with Photofrin-based PDT and discusses the need for post-procedure airway surveillance. The patient also reports he works outdoors and will need clear instructions about light precautions after therapy.
proposed_actions: Consider photodynamic therapy as an option for this medically inoperable early-stage NSCLC that is accessible by bronchoscopy.
After PDT, closely monitor the patient for airway obstruction due to tumour necrosis.
Perform toilet bronchoscopy after endobronchial PDT and repeat as indicated to reduce the risk of bronchial obstruction.
If Photofrin-based PDT is used, advise avoidance of direct sunlight and brightly focused indoor light for at least the photosensitivity period.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient has porphyria or known allergy to porphyrins
Guideline Action: Do not use PDT
Source Statement: There are a number of contraindications for the use of PDT in patients with lung cancer. These include porphyria or known allergies to porphyrins.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Tumor erodes into a major blood vessel
Guideline Action: Do not use PDT
Source Statement: There are a number of contraindications for the use of PDT in patients with lung cancer. These include ... tumours that erode into a major blood vessel.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Existing tracheoesophageal fistula
Guideline Action: Do not use PDT
Source Statement: There are a number of contraindications for the use of PDT in patients with lung cancer. These include ... existing tracheoesophageal fistulas.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient is pregnant, nursing, or a child
Guideline Action: Do not use Photofrin because its use has not been established
Source Statement: Its use in pregnant women, nursing women, and children has not been established, and, therefore, Photofrin® is not recommended for use in those cases (1).

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient receives endobronchial PDT
Guideline Action: Perform toilet bronchoscopy after treatment and repeat as indicated
Source Statement: Toilet bronchoscopies should always be completed following endobronchial PDT to minimize the risk of bronchial obstruction, and the risk of hemoptysis should be considered prior to therapy and monitored post therapy.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient receives PDT
Guideline Action: Monitor closely after the procedure for airway obstruction
Source Statement: Since tumour necrosis can result in post-treatment airway obstruction, patients should be closely monitored after undergoing the procedure and toilet bronchoscopies repeated as indicated.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient receives Photofrin-based PDT
Guideline Action: Advise avoidance of direct sunlight and brightly focused indoor light for at least the photosensitivity period
Source Statement: Patients must avoid exposure of eyes and skin to direct sunlight or brightly focused indoor light.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 60
Recommendation Class: CONDITIONAL_CONSIDER
Activation Condition: Adult patient with primary NSCLC and medically inoperable early-stage disease accessible by bronchoscopy
Guideline Action: Consider photodynamic therapy as an option
Source Statement: Photodynamic therapy could be considered as an option for the treatment of early stage lung cancer in patients with medically inoperable disease that is accessible by bronchoscopy.
proposed_actions_text:
- Consider photodynamic therapy as an option for this medically inoperable early-stage NSCLC that is accessible by bronchoscopy.
- After PDT, closely monitor the patient for airway obstruction due to tumour necrosis.
- Perform toilet bronchoscopy after endobronchial PDT and repeat as indicated to reduce the risk of bronchial obstruction.
- If Photofrin-based PDT is used, advise avoidance of direct sunlight and brightly focused indoor light for at least the photosensitivity period.

patient_vignette: A 29-year-old man underwent right radical orchidectomy for a 2.1 cm pure seminoma confined to the testis, with negative margins and no metastatic disease on staging scans. He asks whether the size of the tumour and the absence of rete testis invasion could justify a less intensive follow-up plan. He is otherwise healthy and is considering options for post-orchidectomy management. After discussion, he is told that surveillance is the preferred approach after orchidectomy, and that management should not be based on a risk-adapted strategy using tumour size or rete testis invasion. He agrees to proceed with surveillance and understands the need for regular follow-up visits and imaging.
proposed_actions: Use surveillance as the preferred post-orchidectomy management strategy.
Do not use a risk-adapted approach as the basis for management.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with stage I testicular seminoma and proposed risk-adapted surveillance based on tumour size or rete testis invasion
Guideline Action: Do not use a risk-adapted approach as the basis for management
Source Statement: Thus, a risk-adapted approach cannot be recommended at this time.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with stage I testicular seminoma after orchidectomy
Guideline Action: Use surveillance as the preferred post-orchidectomy management strategy
Source Statement: The DSG recommends surveillance as the preferred option, because adjuvant therapy is associated with important short and long-term toxicities and second malignancy risks with no evidence of improved survival.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with stage I testicular seminoma on primary surveillance
Guideline Action: Follow a minimum surveillance program with physical examination every 3 to 4 months, chest X-ray every 6 to 12 months, and CT abdomen/pelvis every 3 to 4 months for the first 3 years, then less often thereafter
Source Statement: The minimum surveillance program should be a physical examination every three to four months, chest X-ray every six to twelve months, and computerised tomography (CT) of the abdomen and pelvis every three to four months in the first three years and then less often thereafter.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with stage I testicular seminoma on primary surveillance
Guideline Action: Include follow-up investigations of sites at risk of relapse
Source Statement: In addition, follow-up should include appropriate investigations of sites at risk of relapse.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with stage I testicular seminoma who prefers immediate treatment or is unsuitable for primary surveillance
Guideline Action: Offer adjuvant radiotherapy
Source Statement: For patients who prefer immediate treatment, or who are unsuitable for primary surveillance, adjuvant RT is the recommended option.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with stage I testicular seminoma receiving adjuvant radiotherapy
Guideline Action: Use a radiotherapy dose of at least 20 Gy and no more than 30 Gy
Source Statement: When adjuvant RT is the preferred option, a radiation dose of at least 20 Gy and no more than 30 Gy is recommended.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with stage I testicular seminoma treated with adjuvant therapy
Guideline Action: Continue post-treatment monitoring for relapse; except after extended-field radiotherapy, follow-up should be as thorough as surveillance
Source Statement: In patients treated with adjuvant therapy, post treatment monitoring for disease relapse is still necessary. Except in the specific case of extended-field radiotherapy, the follow-up after adjuvant therapy should be as thorough as the surveillance conducted in the absence of adjuvant therapy.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: GENERAL
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: adult patient with stage I testicular seminoma and planned adjuvant therapy with fertility concerns
Guideline Action: Offer sperm banking; if radiotherapy is used, offer scrotal shielding
Source Statement: If adjuvant therapy is planned, sperm banking (and scrotal shielding with RT) should be offered if future fertility is of concern to the patient.
proposed_actions_text:
- Use surveillance as the preferred post-orchidectomy management strategy.
- Do not use a risk-adapted approach as the basis for management.

patient_vignette: A 58-year-old man is newly diagnosed with IgG multiple myeloma after presenting with fatigue, anemia, lytic bone lesions, and mild hypercalcemia. He has ECOG performance status 1, normal creatinine, and no major comorbidities. After hydration and initial remission-induction chemotherapy, his disease burden has improved. He asks whether he should undergo stem cell transplant and whether he should receive a planned second transplant because a friend told him that “more is better.” His oncologist is also considering adding thalidomide to the upfront regimen and wonders if stem cells should be purged before collection. Stem cells have not yet been collected.
proposed_actions: Proceed with autologous transplantation; evidence is strongest in patients under 65 years old without significant renal dysfunction after hydration and remission-induction chemotherapy.
Refer for transplant assessment early and avoid extensive exposure to alkylating agents such as melphalan before stem cell collection.
Do not recommend planned tandem transplantation outside a clinical trial.
Use thalidomide only in the context of a clinical trial.
Do not use currently available stem cell purging methods routinely.
rules_text:
Rule ID: R001
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Considering routine allogeneic transplantation for multiple myeloma
Guideline Action: Do not recommend allogeneic transplantation as routine therapy
Source Statement: There is insufficient evidence to recommend allogeneic transplantation as routine therapy for multiple myeloma.

Rule ID: R002
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient may be eligible for transplantation and stem cells have not yet been collected
Guideline Action: Avoid extensive exposure to alkylating agents such as melphalan before stem cell collection
Source Statement: Patients who are potentially eligible for transplantation should be referred for transplant assessment early after diagnosis and should not be given extensive exposure to alkylating agents such as melphalan prior to the collection of stem cells.

Rule ID: R003
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Considering tandem transplantation outside a clinical trial
Guideline Action: Do not use two transplants performed in succession outside a clinical trial
Source Statement: There is insufficient evidence to recommend a treatment plan that includes two transplants performed in succession (tandem transplantation) outside of a clinical trial.

Rule ID: R004
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Newly diagnosed patient is being considered for thalidomide
Guideline Action: Use thalidomide only in the context of a clinical trial
Source Statement: Thalidomide should only be used in newly diagnosed patients in the context of a clinical trial.

Rule ID: R005
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Considering planned double (tandem) autologous stem cell transplantation
Guideline Action: Do not recommend planned tandem ASCT on current evidence
Source Statement: Planned double (tandem) ASCT cannot be recommended on the current evidence.

Rule ID: R006
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 80
Recommendation Class: NOT_RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Considering purging or selection of harvested stem cells
Guideline Action: Do not use currently available purging methods routinely
Source Statement: Currently available methods of purging have not demonstrated clinical benefit and are not, therefore, recommended.

Rule ID: R007
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Adult patient with advanced-stage multiple myeloma and good performance status
Guideline Action: Recommend autologous transplantation
Source Statement: Autologous transplantation is recommended for patients with advanced-stage myeloma and good performance status.

Rule ID: R008
Scenario: INITIAL_DIAGNOSIS
Priority: 70
Recommendation Class: RECOMMENDED
Activation Condition: Patient is under 65 years old, has no significant renal dysfunction, and has had hydration and remission-induction chemotherapy
Guideline Action: Proceed with autologous transplantation; evidence is strongest in this group
Source Statement: The evidence is strongest for patients under 65 years of age without significant renal dysfunction following hydration and remission-induction chemotherapy.
proposed_actions_text:
- Proceed with autologous transplantation; evidence is strongest in patients under 65 years old without significant renal dysfunction after hydration and remission-induction chemotherapy.
- Refer for transplant assessment early and avoid extensive exposure to alkylating agents such as melphalan before stem cell collection.
- Do not recommend planned tandem transplantation outside a clinical trial.
- Use thalidomide only in the context of a clinical trial.
- Do not use currently available stem cell purging methods routinely.
